L1.area: Bundled browser engine patches and GPU rendering pipeline

L2.description: A heap buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the GPU command processing layer where texture dimension validation uses incorrect bounds checking. A remote attacker who has compromised the renderer process could potentially perform a sandbox escape by triggering out-of-bounds memory access through a crafted HTML page.

L3.area: Chromium GPU command buffer service patches for texture validation
L3.description: A heap buffer overflow vulnerability exists where GPU command validation routines use generic target-type-based bounds checking for texture operations instead of querying the actual texture object's valid mip levels. This allows out-of-bounds memory access when processing GPU commands related to framebuffer texture attachment, compressed texture upload, and texture storage allocation, potentially enabling a sandbox escape from a compromised renderer process.

ground_truth.reason: The GPU command buffer service uses ValidForTarget with generic target-based max mip level checks instead of ValidForTextureTarget which queries the actual texture's valid mip levels, allowing out-of-bounds heap memory access via crafted GPU commands from a compromised renderer.
